sub_competencies.name: Connect
sub_competencies.definition: Contribute and connect others to Red Hat's communities and shared purpose.
sub_competencies.description: Building and nurturing connections within and beyond Red Hat, linking people to communities, resources, and shared purpose to strengthen the collective impact of the organization.
indicators: Connect others to Red Hat communities and resources
Participate actively in communities of practice
Help new associates build their network
Link people with shared interests or complementary skills
Foster a sense of belonging and shared purpose

sub_competencies.name: Extend Trust
sub_competencies.definition: Show confidence in the ability of others to contribute.
sub_competencies.description: Demonstrating belief in colleagues' capabilities by delegating meaningful work, sharing information openly, and giving others the autonomy to make decisions and contribute in their own way.
indicators: Delegate tasks with confidence in others' abilities
Give others autonomy to make decisions
Assume positive intent in interactions
Share information and context freely
Empower others to take ownership of their work

sub_competencies.name: Be Transparent
sub_competencies.definition: Openly share information and intentions.
sub_competencies.description: Sharing information, decisions, and rationale openly to foster trust and enable informed participation. Transparency builds credibility and creates an environment where associates feel informed and empowered.
indicators: Share information proactively and openly
Communicate reasoning behind decisions
Make processes and criteria visible to others
Acknowledge mistakes and uncertainties
Provide context so others can contribute effectively

sub_competencies.name: Collaborate
sub_competencies.definition: Invite cooperation and productive dialogue to create better solutions.
sub_competencies.description: Actively seeking input from diverse perspectives, fostering productive dialogue, and working together across teams and functions to produce better outcomes than any individual could achieve alone.
indicators: Seek diverse perspectives when solving problems
Create opportunities for cross-functional teamwork
Build on others' ideas constructively
Facilitate productive group discussions
Share credit and recognize collective contributions

sub_competencies.name: Promote Inclusive Meritocracy
sub_competencies.definition: Empower others to contribute ideas and value solutions based on their merit.
sub_competencies.description: Creating an environment where the best ideas win regardless of their source, ensuring all voices are heard, and valuing contributions based on quality and impact rather than hierarchy or tenure.
indicators: Evaluate ideas on merit rather than source
Actively solicit input from underrepresented voices
Create safe spaces for diverse perspectives
Challenge decisions that exclude valid contributions
Recognize and amplify ideas from all levels

sub_competencies.name: Practice Strategic Agility
sub_competencies.definition: Create and continuously refine plans that capitalize on emerging business opportunities.
sub_competencies.description: Developing flexible strategies that adapt to changing conditions, anticipating market shifts, and adjusting plans to seize emerging opportunities while maintaining alignment with long-term goals.
indicators: Adapt plans based on new information or market changes
Identify emerging opportunities and threats
Balance short-term execution with long-term vision
Develop contingency plans for multiple scenarios
Continuously reassess and refine strategic direction

sub_competencies.name: Apply Business Perspective
sub_competencies.definition: Shape decisions using an understanding of Red Hat and our current business environment.
sub_competencies.description: Incorporating knowledge of Red Hat's business model, competitive landscape, and market dynamics into day-to-day decisions to ensure alignment with organizational goals and customer needs.
indicators: Consider business impact when making decisions
Stay informed about industry trends and competition
Align team goals with organizational strategy
Evaluate trade-offs using business context
Connect technical decisions to business outcomes

sub_competencies.name: Generate a Vision
sub_competencies.definition: Explore and share inspiring possibilities.
sub_competencies.description: Looking beyond current constraints to envision future possibilities, articulating a compelling direction that motivates others, and creating alignment around a shared aspiration for what could be achieved.
indicators: Articulate a compelling future direction
Inspire others with possibilities beyond the status quo
Think beyond immediate constraints
Connect daily work to a larger purpose
Help others see how their contributions matter

sub_competencies.name: Navigate Complex Connections
sub_competencies.definition: Build awareness of the interrelated role of processes, departments, and individuals.
sub_competencies.description: Understanding how different parts of the organization interconnect and influence each other, identifying dependencies and relationships, and leveraging this awareness to improve coordination and outcomes.
indicators: Understand cross-functional dependencies
Identify how changes in one area affect others
Build relationships across organizational boundaries
Facilitate coordination between interdependent teams
Map stakeholders and their interests

sub_competencies.name: Create Context
sub_competencies.definition: Share information and ideas that align people to a common vision.
sub_competencies.description: Providing the background, rationale, and framing that help others understand the 'why' behind decisions and directions, enabling alignment and informed action across the organization.
indicators: Provide background and rationale for decisions
Frame information to help others understand priorities
Connect individual work to broader organizational goals
Ensure consistent messaging across teams
Help others understand how their work fits in

sub_competencies.name: Listen
sub_competencies.definition: Work to understand the meaning and motivation of what others are communicating.
sub_competencies.description: Going beyond hearing words to truly understanding the intent, concerns, and motivations behind what others communicate, using active listening techniques to build understanding and trust.
indicators: Practice active listening with full attention
Ask clarifying questions to deepen understanding
Paraphrase to confirm understanding
Pick up on non-verbal cues and emotions
Listen to understand rather than to respond

sub_competencies.name: Build Relationships
sub_competencies.definition: Establish trustworthy connections that lead to expanded reach.
sub_competencies.description: Proactively developing and maintaining authentic professional relationships that create mutual value, expand networks, and enable collaboration across organizational boundaries.
indicators: Develop genuine professional connections
Maintain and nurture existing relationships
Build trust through consistent follow-through
Expand network across functions and levels
Create mutually beneficial partnerships

sub_competencies.name: Commit to Common Goals
sub_competencies.definition: Identify and stay focused on common goals.
sub_competencies.description: Finding shared objectives that unite diverse stakeholders, maintaining focus on collective outcomes, and balancing individual priorities with team and organizational goals.
indicators: Identify shared objectives across teams
Align personal goals with team priorities
Support decisions that serve the greater good
Stay focused on collective outcomes
Help resolve conflicts by refocusing on shared goals

sub_competencies.name: Engage in Dialogue
sub_competencies.definition: Objectively add productive ideas to the conversation.
sub_competencies.description: Contributing constructive, well-reasoned ideas to discussions while remaining open to different perspectives, fostering productive debate, and moving conversations toward actionable outcomes.
indicators: Contribute constructive ideas to discussions
Present viewpoints with supporting reasoning
Remain open to opposing perspectives
Move conversations toward actionable outcomes
Challenge ideas respectfully and constructively

sub_competencies.name: Take Initiative
sub_competencies.definition: Act on opportunities to add value.
sub_competencies.description: Proactively identifying opportunities to contribute, stepping up to fill gaps, and taking action without waiting for direction when you see a way to create value for the team or organization.
indicators: Identify and act on opportunities proactively
Volunteer for challenging assignments
Step up to fill gaps without being asked
Propose solutions rather than just identifying problems
Go beyond assigned responsibilities when appropriate

sub_competencies.name: Prioritize
sub_competencies.definition: Concentrate on the most important tasks first.
sub_competencies.description: Effectively evaluating competing demands, focusing energy on highest-impact activities, and making deliberate choices about what to do and what to defer or decline.
indicators: Focus on highest-impact tasks first
Make deliberate trade-off decisions
Manage time and resources effectively
Communicate priorities clearly to others
Say no to lower-priority requests when appropriate

sub_competencies.name: Focus on Results
sub_competencies.definition: Continuously progress towards desired outcomes.
sub_competencies.description: Maintaining consistent forward momentum toward goals, tracking progress, removing obstacles, and adjusting approaches as needed to deliver meaningful outcomes.
indicators: Set clear, measurable goals
Track progress and adjust course as needed
Remove obstacles to achieving outcomes
Maintain momentum despite setbacks
Deliver on commitments consistently

sub_competencies.name: Embrace Change
sub_competencies.definition: Expect change and adapt to remain agile.
sub_competencies.description: Approaching change with a positive and flexible mindset, helping others navigate transitions, and treating evolving circumstances as opportunities for improvement rather than disruptions.
indicators: Adapt quickly to changing circumstances
Maintain effectiveness during transitions
Help others navigate change positively
See change as an opportunity for improvement
Remain flexible in approach and methods

sub_competencies.name: Persevere
sub_competencies.definition: Pursue goals even in the face of obstacles.
sub_competencies.description: Demonstrating resilience and determination in the face of challenges, setbacks, and adversity, maintaining focus on goals and finding alternative paths when initial approaches are blocked.
indicators: Maintain focus despite obstacles and setbacks
Find alternative approaches when blocked
Demonstrate resilience under pressure
Stay motivated through difficult periods
Learn from failures and keep moving forward

sub_competencies.name: Focus on Team
sub_competencies.definition: Place the success of the team ahead of your own.
sub_competencies.description: Prioritizing team outcomes over individual recognition, supporting teammates, sharing knowledge and resources, and contributing to a collaborative team culture where collective success is valued.
indicators: Put team success above personal recognition
Support teammates in achieving their goals
Share knowledge and resources freely
Contribute to positive team culture
Step in to help when teammates need support

sub_competencies.name: Encourage Others
sub_competencies.definition: Support and energize others to do their best.
sub_competencies.description: Showing appreciation, providing praise, expressing confidence in others' abilities, and celebrating accomplishments to sustain motivation, engagement, and enthusiasm across the team.
indicators: Provide genuine praise and recognition
Express confidence in others' abilities
Celebrate team and individual accomplishments
Motivate others during challenging times
Create a culture of appreciation and recognition

sub_competencies.name: Amplify Strengths
sub_competencies.definition: Help others recognize and use their strengths.
sub_competencies.description: Identifying and highlighting the unique strengths of team members, creating opportunities for people to leverage what they do best, and fostering a culture that values strengths-based development.
indicators: Identify and articulate others' strengths
Create opportunities for people to use their strengths
Encourage strengths-based development conversations
Delegate work to align with team members' strengths
Model self-awareness by sharing your own strengths

sub_competencies.name: Share Feedback
sub_competencies.definition: Provide information to others about their behavior, skill, and impact.
sub_competencies.description: Offering timely, specific, and constructive feedback to help others grow, sharing both positive reinforcement and developmental observations in a way that builds trust and supports improvement.
indicators: Provide timely, specific feedback
Balance positive and constructive feedback
Share feedback with respect and good intent
Use concrete examples when giving feedback
Create regular opportunities for feedback exchange

sub_competencies.name: Receive Feedback
sub_competencies.definition: Consider and evaluate feedback from others to improve contribution.
sub_competencies.description: Actively seeking feedback, listening without defensiveness, thoughtfully evaluating input, and demonstrating growth by incorporating feedback into behavior and work practices.
indicators: Proactively ask for feedback from others
Listen to feedback without defensiveness
Incorporate feedback into behavior and work
Follow up to show how feedback was applied
Use frameworks like Stop-Start-Continue to invite input

sub_competencies.name: Troubleshoot
sub_competencies.definition: Probe to identify the root cause of the problem.
sub_competencies.description: Using a methodical, systematic approach to isolate and identify the true source of problems, ensuring the right problem is identified before investing time and energy in developing solutions.
indicators: Ask probing questions to understand the problem
Use systematic methods to isolate root causes
Document issues thoroughly for follow-up
Distinguish symptoms from underlying causes
Conduct audits to proactively identify potential problems

sub_competencies.name: Think Critically
sub_competencies.definition: Objectively analyze information from multiple perspectives.
sub_competencies.description: Navigating disparate and sometimes conflicting information, synthesizing complex ideas from multiple viewpoints, and presenting a clear, cohesive picture to drive common understanding and better decisions.
indicators: Analyze information from multiple perspectives
Synthesize complex, sometimes conflicting data
Create common fact bases for shared understanding
Evaluate options with pros and cons
Lead brainstorming and problem-solving discussions

sub_competencies.name: Apply Data
sub_competencies.definition: Use data to inform solutions.
sub_competencies.description: Understanding what data is needed, extracting and managing it effectively, and assessing information to inform and direct potential solutions with context and clarity.
indicators: Identify relevant data sources for decision-making
Analyze data to identify patterns and insights
Use data to support or challenge assumptions
Present data-driven recommendations clearly
Balance data analysis with timely action

sub_competencies.name: Take Appropriate Risk
sub_competencies.definition: Weigh risk against the benefit to support decisions.
sub_competencies.description: Making timely, fact-based decisions using relevant data and personal judgment, recognizing when situations need additional input versus immediate action, and balancing agility with consideration for potential risks.
indicators: Conduct risk-benefit analyses for important decisions
Identify potential risks proactively
Make timely decisions with appropriate due diligence
Discuss risks and benefits before taking action
Learn from post-mortem discussions to improve future decisions

sub_competencies.name: Adopt Customer Perspective
sub_competencies.definition: Take the perspective of the customer by fully understanding their business.
sub_competencies.description: Understanding your customers' situation, needs, and priorities by listening, asking questions, and thinking from their point of view rather than starting from your own perspective.
indicators: Understand who your customers are and what they value
Ask questions to build understanding of customer needs
Research customer industries and business contexts
Think from the customer perspective when designing solutions
Represent the voice of the customer in decisions

sub_competencies.name: Anticipate Needs
sub_competencies.definition: Apply knowledge and insight to foresee business needs.
sub_competencies.description: Proactively engaging with customers to understand evolving needs, using active listening and critical thinking to identify unexpressed requirements, and preparing for future challenges before they arise.
indicators: Engage regularly with customers to understand evolving needs
Identify unexpressed needs through active listening
Stay informed about industry trends affecting customers
Develop proactive solutions before problems arise
Build long-term strategies based on anticipated needs

sub_competencies.name: Go Above and Beyond
sub_competencies.definition: Create experiences and outcomes that exceed customer expectations.
sub_competencies.description: Seeking to do more than the minimum by recommending improvements, developing resources, and creating experiences that deliver exceptional value and delight customers beyond their expectations.
indicators: Exceed expectations in customer interactions
Recommend improvements to products and processes
Develop resources that enhance customer experience
Address gaps in team ability to serve customers
Foster a culture of passionate customer service

sub_competencies.name: Respect Relationships
sub_competencies.definition: Consider the customer relationship as an important outcome.
sub_competencies.description: Building and maintaining trustworthy partnerships with customers, consistently delivering value, and treating the relationship itself as a key outcome alongside project deliverables.
indicators: Build and maintain trusting customer partnerships
Follow through on commitments consistently
Communicate early and often with customers
Value long-term relationships over short-term gains
Act as a trusted adviser to customers

sub_competencies.name: Resolve Issues
sub_competencies.definition: Take responsibility for thoroughly resolving customer issues.
sub_competencies.description: Taking ownership of customer problems, resolving them thoroughly rather than superficially, and thinking holistically about how to prevent issues from recurring in the future.
indicators: Take ownership of customer issues end-to-end
Listen actively to understand customer complaints
Identify root causes rather than applying surface fixes
Proactively audit processes to prevent future issues
Collaborate with teammates to resolve complex problems

sub_competencies.name: Recognize Learning Opportunities
sub_competencies.definition: Explore various approaches to learning in your current role.
sub_competencies.description: Identifying learning potential in everyday experiences -- special assignments, new connections, volunteering, and cross-functional interactions -- rather than waiting for formal training opportunities.
indicators: Find learning opportunities in everyday work
Seek stretch assignments and new challenges
Build cross-functional connections for broader learning
Track and reflect on daily learning experiences
Ask for developmental opportunities proactively

sub_competencies.name: Practice
sub_competencies.definition: Use knowledge across various situations to develop a skill.
sub_competencies.description: Deliberately practicing new skills and behaviors with a growth mindset, seeking feedback, and persevering through difficulty to build proficiency and career mobility.
indicators: Deliberately practice new skills in safe environments
Challenge past habits to try new approaches
Seek feedback on practice efforts
Demonstrate grit by persisting through difficulty
Apply learning from one context to new situations

sub_competencies.name: Experiment
sub_competencies.definition: Explore through trial and error.
sub_competencies.description: Learning from firsthand experience by trying new approaches, taking calculated risks, running quick inexpensive experiments, and treating failures as valuable learning data rather than setbacks.
indicators: Try new approaches rather than defaulting to familiar methods
Run quick, low-cost experiments to test ideas
Learn from mistakes rather than avoiding similar situations
Consider problems from opposite perspectives
Embrace failure as a path to innovation

sub_competencies.name: Reflect
sub_competencies.definition: Examine your actions and impact.
sub_competencies.description: Making time to think carefully about everyday actions, emotions, and thoughts, identifying patterns in successes and failures, and using these insights to refine behaviors and practices.
indicators: Regularly examine actions and their outcomes
Look for patterns across successes and failures
Seek feedback as data for reflective analysis
Practice thoughtful sequencing of events and responses
Develop self-awareness through ongoing reflection

sub_competencies.name: Share Expertise
sub_competencies.definition: Share what you know with others.
sub_competencies.description: Contributing to collective knowledge by teaching, mentoring, sharing feedback, and delegating developmental tasks, recognizing that every associate has unique perspectives worth sharing.
indicators: Teach skills and knowledge to colleagues
Mentor new associates or onboarding buddies
Share key takeaways from conferences and events
Delegate developmental tasks to help others grow
Provide balanced, constructive feedback to peers
